title: hieracium laetifolium
intro: Hieracium laetifolium, also known as Kukuihelani, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family that is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its saponin and sesquiterpene lactone content. The plant has been used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, and is also believed to have anti-cancer properties. Hieracium laetifolium can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
